title: 1.1: Tests basic lock by acquiring, holding for a non-zero duration, then releasing
1.3: Tests two simultaneous lock attempts on the same resource where the second blocks until the first releases
1.4: Tests three concurrent lock attempts on the same resource using goroutines
1.5: The three-lock test uses synchronization to wait for all goroutines to complete
1.7: Tests that two different resources can be locked simultaneously without blocking
2.1: Places all distributed locking tests in the internal/dsync directory
3.1: All tests use Go's standard testing package with *testing.T parameter
3.2: All tests follow the naming pattern Test<Description> with capital T prefix
3.3: All tests use the NewDRWMutex function to create mutex instances
3.4: All tests use context.Background() when calling Unlock
4.1: Concurrent lock tests use goroutines with appropriate synchronization primitives
